content.text: The PO-33's step sequencer lets you record and arrange patterns using real-time recording or step-by-step entry. Each pattern can be up to 16 steps long, and patterns can be chained together for longer arrangements.
The play button starts and stops pattern playback. When playing, the current step is shown on the display and the pads light up to indicate triggered sounds.

content.caption: Play button with orange ring accent

content.text: The BPM knob controls the playback tempo from 60 to 240 BPM. The current tempo is displayed when the knob is turned.

content.title: Fine Tempo
content.text: Hold the BPM knob pressed and turn the sound knob for fine tempo adjustment in smaller increments.
The meter knob (M) sets the number of steps in a pattern. Options include 16, 12, 8, and 4 steps.
Record patterns by playing the pads while the sequencer runs. Notes are recorded with their timing intact.

items: Press play to start the sequencer
Press record (it will flash to show armed)
Play the pads in time with the beat
Press record again to stop recording

content.title: Overdubbing
content.text: Recording adds to existing pattern content. To erase and start fresh, hold write + record to clear the pattern first.
Write mode lets you enter notes step-by-step for precise pattern programming.

items: Press write to enter write mode
Turn sound knob to navigate to the desired step (1-16)
Press pads to add sounds at the current step
Multiple sounds can be added to the same step
Press write again to exit write mode

content.text: To remove notes from a pattern:

items: In write mode: Hold write + press the pad to delete that sound from all steps
During playback: Hold record + press pad at the moment you want to erase
Clear entire pattern: Hold write + press record
